sourceMovie.title: Spider-Man
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Spider-Man (2002) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Sam Raimi's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Amazing Spider-Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spider-Man clicked for you, The Amazing Spider-Man (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spider-Man clicked for you, The Amazing Spider-Man 2 (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Spider-Man?
faq.answer: Try The Amazing Spider-Man, The Amazing Spider-Man 2, The Incredible Hulk—each shares DNA with Spider-Man while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Spider-Man?
faq.answer: Spider-Man (2002) is best described as Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Spider-Man part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Spider-Man Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
